rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LINDA YEE LIN MA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #3, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) The interests of Executor's three minor children have never been represented in these proceedings. 2) It appears that Executor commingled estate funds with her personal funds. 3) Subparagraph d. of paragraph 15 of petitions needs further clarification. Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROBERT COFFEY
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] Administrator only met with a bank representative on 5/1/25 to inquire about the two large, unknown disbursements on 3/14/23. No Letters have been filed for Cynthia Coggin-Antone as the personal reprsentative of the estate of Dana Talbert Coggin. Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. (A petitioner does not need to give herself notice. [Probate Code Section 1201.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SHERALYN RITA WEBER
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, although the statutory compensation was calculated using net proceeds instead of the sale price of the real property (which would have amounted to greater compensation to the administrator and her attorney), the loss on disposal of the tangible personal property ($500) was not included in the fee base, all of the cash is in excess of the FDIC insured amounts is at one bank, and the receipt attached as Exhibit F to the petition does not identify any persons (name of the decedent nor the person who paid the bill).

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LILLIE MAE TYLER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SACHIN SHASHIKANT VORA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/5/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially with a cover sheet. (2) When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor through whom they take (or would have taken) shall be named and his or her date of death and relationship to the decedent shall be stated. [LRSF 14.18 C.] Who were the decedent's grandparents and parents, and when did they die? (3) The petitioner, the person named in item 2 of the petition, is missing from item 8 of the petition. (4) No nominations were attached to the petition as Attachment 3g(2)(b). Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 10/22/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SHARON LEATRICE THOMPSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION TO DETERMINE SUCCESSION TO PRIMARY RESIDENCE

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LOLITA C. ARCIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/17/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) In the estate of Estelita B. Pasillao, the records seem to indicate that she was also survived by the issue of a predeceased child. This petition must be supplemented again to address this. (2) That heir must also be given notice. (3) The wrong form of notice was served to give notice of the amendment, and instructions for how to appear remotely were not attached to that Notice of Hearing. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 11/3/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Daughter Estelita B. Pasillao post-deceased, so the petition should have disclosed who her successor-in-interest is. Because that case is also here (as very subtly disclosed in the family tree attached to the supplement filed on 8/21/25), the examiner was able to review that case to determine that this petitioner was appointed as the administrator of that estate. The family tree, however, is very misleading, as the lines that should go just from the parent to his/her issue, or between an heir and his/her spouse, connect siblings to each other and this decedent directly to one or both grandchildren.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FERNANDO CANALES BECERRA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/06/2025. These issues must be cleared/addressed by the petitioner's verified supplement to the petition two weeks before the next hearing date: (1) The real property has been held as joint tenants. Petition needs to provide an authority under which the court can negate joint tenancy and grant this petition. Joint tenancy would not be a part of the estate as it passes by right of survivorship. (2) Petition to set forth all known heirs of the decedent. Prob. Code sec. 13651(a)(5). (3) Notice of hearing must be given to those heirs. sec. 13655(a)(2). Notice of Directions for Remote Appearance and Access must be given. LRSF 14.1.C.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CLEVELAND GALLAT
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/17/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proof of publication has been filed yet. (2) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (3) If the petitioner has not yet been appointed as the personal representative of the post-deceased beneficiary's estate, she lacks standing. (4) When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor through whom they take (or would have taken) shall be named and his or her date of death and relationship to the decedent shall be stated. [LRSF 14.18 C.] Who were the decedent's parents and when did they die? (5) Who is the successor-in-interest to Michael Gallat, III, who also post-deceased? Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 11/3/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
